title: 6.0 Hydrocarbons and their Natural Sources
description: Quiz covering Unit 6 of Grade 10 Chemistry: organic chemistry history, alkanes, alkenes, alkynes, aromatic hydrocarbons, benzene, and natural sources of hydrocarbons.

questions.question: According to Vitalism theory, organic compounds could only be produced by:
options: living organisms through a vital force
questions.correctAnswer: living organisms through a vital force

questions.question: Friedrich Wöhler synthesized urea in 1828 by evaporating an aqueous solution of:
Organic chemistry is the study of carbon compounds. Which class of compounds is considered inorganic?
The ability of carbon atoms to join together to form short or long chains and rings is called:
In a bond-line structural representation, what is represented by the line junctions?
A functional group is best defined as:
options: the longest carbon chain in a molecule
a group of atoms that determines the chemical behavior of a molecule
any atom other than carbon or hydrogen
the repeating unit in a homologous series
questions.correctAnswer: a group of atoms that determines the chemical behavior of a molecule

questions.question: Which class of organic compounds lacks a specific functional group?
Saturated hydrocarbons are those that contain:
options: only single bonds between carbon atoms
questions.correctAnswer: only single bonds between carbon atoms

questions.question: A homologous series is characterized by each consecutive member differing by:
Which characteristic is absent from members of a homologous series?
At room temperature, the first four members of the alkane homologous series are:
All alkanes have densities that are:
options: equal to the density of water at 4 °C

questions.question: As the number of carbon atoms in an alkane increases, its boiling point generally:
The prefix used in common names when all carbon atoms form a continuous chain is:
In IUPAC nomenclature, what does the suffix -ane indicate?
In the IUPAC name 3-methylheptane, the locant '3' indicates:
options: the number of carbon atoms in the parent chain
the position of the substituent on the parent chain
the total number of substituent groups
the position of the double bond
questions.correctAnswer: the position of the substituent on the parent chain

questions.question: When naming branched-chain alkanes, the parent chain is selected as:
options: the longest continuous chain of carbon atoms
the chain with the most substituents
the shortest chain containing all substituents
questions.correctAnswer: the longest continuous chain of carbon atoms

questions.question: Removing one hydrogen atom from an alkane produces:
Alkyl groups have names ending in:
Structural isomers are compounds that have:
options: different molecular formulas but the same structure
the same molecular formula but different connectivity of atoms
the same structure but different physical properties only
different numbers of carbon and hydrogen atoms
questions.correctAnswer: the same molecular formula but different connectivity of atoms

questions.question: How many structural isomers does butane (C₄H₁₀) have?
The Würtz reaction involves the reaction of a halogenated alkane with:
In the decarboxylation method for preparing alkanes, the reactant is:
options: a sodium salt of a carboxylic acid with soda lime
questions.correctAnswer: a sodium salt of a carboxylic acid with soda lime

questions.question: Alkanes are sometimes referred to as paraffins, which means:
Which type of reaction is most characteristic of alkanes?
The halogenation of methane in the presence of light proceeds through:
An elimination reaction in alkanes involves:
options: addition of hydrogen across a double bond
removal of smaller molecules leading to multiple bond formation
replacement of hydrogen by a halogen
reaction with oxygen to produce carbon dioxide and water
questions.correctAnswer: removal of smaller molecules leading to multiple bond formation

questions.question: Cycloalkanes that contain a single ring have the general formula:
The simplest alicyclic compound is:
Alkanes are primarily used as:
Alkenes are also known as:
The general formula for an alkene is:
Ethene occurs naturally as:
options: a plant hormone involved in fruit ripening
questions.correctAnswer: a plant hormone involved in fruit ripening

questions.question: The IUPAC name for the compound with common name ethylene is:
The common name for 2-methylpropene is:
When numbering an alkene chain for IUPAC naming, the numbering should:
options: begin at the end nearer the double bond
begin at the end with more substituents
begin at the end farther from the double bond
questions.correctAnswer: begin at the end nearer the double bond

questions.question: In addition to chain isomerism, alkenes exhibit:
The cis and trans prefixes in geometrical isomerism refer to:
options: the length of the carbon chain
the spatial arrangement of groups about the double bond
the position of substituents on a ring
questions.correctAnswer: the spatial arrangement of groups about the double bond

questions.question: Which of the following can exhibit cis-trans isomerism?
Geometrical isomerism exists in alkenes because:
options: rotation about the double bond is restricted
the double bond is easily broken
questions.correctAnswer: rotation about the double bond is restricted

questions.question: One laboratory method for preparing alkenes involves:
options: dehydration of alcohols with concentrated H₂SO₄
reaction of alkyl halides with sodium
questions.correctAnswer: dehydration of alcohols with concentrated H₂SO₄

questions.question: The main type of reaction that alkenes undergo is:
When bromine in CCl₄ is added to an alkene, the reddish-brown colour disappears because:
options: bromine adds across the double bond
questions.correctAnswer: bromine adds across the double bond

questions.question: Markovnikov's rule states that when an alkene reacts with HX, the hydrogen adds to:
options: the carbon with fewer hydrogen atoms
the carbon with the greater number of hydrogen atoms
the carbon atom in the middle of the chain
questions.correctAnswer: the carbon with the greater number of hydrogen atoms

questions.question: The addition of water to an alkene in the presence of an acid catalyst produces:
Alkaline KMnO₄ solution used to test for unsaturation is called:
The polymerization of ethene under high pressure and temperature produces:
Cycloalkenes have the general formula:
The general formula for the alkyne homologous series is:
The simplest alkyne is commonly known as:
Alkynes show which type of isomerism?
Ethyne can be prepared in the laboratory by the reaction of calcium carbide with:
The combustion of alkynes produces a smoky luminous flame due to:
Partial hydrogenation of an alkyne in the presence of Lindlar's catalyst gives:
When acetylene is heated at 600–700 °C, it undergoes trimerization to form:
Acetylene is stored as a solution in acetone in steel cylinders because:
options: it is a liquid at room temperature
it is unstable and explosive when compressed alone
questions.correctAnswer: it is unstable and explosive when compressed alone

questions.question: Oxy-acetylene torches produce flame temperatures of about:
The term 'aromatic' originally referred to compounds with:
Benzene has a molecular formula of:
The structure of benzene was first proposed by:
The carbon-carbon bonds in benzene are best described as:
options: intermediate between single and double bonds
questions.correctAnswer: intermediate between single and double bonds

questions.question: In the IUPAC system, methylbenzene is commonly known as:
In disubstituted benzenes, the prefix ortho- (o-) refers to substituents located at positions:
Benzene is a carcinogenic substance that can cause:
Benzene fails to decolorize which of the following reagents?
The characteristic reaction of benzene is:
Natural gas consists chiefly of:
Crude oil mainly contains which classes of hydrocarbons?
The octane number of 2,2,4-trimethylpentane (isooctane) is:
Destructive distillation of coal in the absence of air produces coal tar, coal gas, and:
